description: Single source of truth for all registered work streams. Add a new entry here — and create the matching folder structure — to register a new work stream. Schemas and portal tooling read this file to build workstream dropdown lists and route assets.

workstreams.label: Architects
workstreams.description: Solution architects and enterprise architects working on design, ADRs, trade-off analysis, and technical proposals.

workstreams.label: Capacity Management
workstreams.description: Engineers responsible for demand forecasting, resource sizing, utilization analysis, and cost optimisation.

workstreams.label: Developers
workstreams.description: Software engineers focused on code generation, review, refactoring, documentation, and testing.

workstreams.label: Performance Engineers
workstreams.description: Engineers who design and run load tests, analyse profiles, and drive performance improvements.

workstreams.label: Site Reliability Engineers
workstreams.description: SREs responsible for incident response, runbooks, alerting, SLOs, and reliability automation.

workstreams.label: System Designers
workstreams.description: Engineers who produce system design documents, sequence diagrams, API contracts, and data models.

workstreams.label: Testers
workstreams.description: QA engineers who generate test cases, analyse coverage gaps, write exploratory charters, and automate test suites.

workstreams.label: Data Analytics
workstreams.description: Data analysts, data scientists, and BI professionals who transform raw data into actionable business insights through statistical analysis, trend detection, and forecasting.

adding_new_workstream: See docs/adding-workstreams.md for the step-by-step guide.
